name: Curtis Fuller
abstract: Curtis DuBois Fuller (December 15, 1932 – May 8, 2021) was an American jazz trombonist. He was a member of Art Blakey's Jazz Messengers and contributed to many classic jazz recordings.

interviews.title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Toshiko Akiyoshi and Friends

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I got Curtis Fuller and an unknown piano from Philadelphia named McCoy Tyner. That was the first group... Curtis Fuller said, 'It’s a sextet. We’re playing jazz. Why don’t we call it the Jazztet?'
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: The trombone section was a very, very good section with Åke Persson playing lead, Melba Liston, Curtis Fuller, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: And Art picked up on it, and when he put his touch on it, it was ready to go. ... those guys, Freddie Hubbard, Curtis Fuller and Wayne Shorter, they ate it up like it was Post Toasties. They played it better than I could.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Golson took you to San Francisco for a three-week stand at the Jazz Workshop with Curtis Fuller... this turned into your first important engagement because in 1959, this lead to the Jazztet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: We started doing a group called the Timeless Allstars, which was put together by Cedar [Walton] and Harold, Curtis Fuller, Billy, Buster, traveling all over Europe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: We’ve played together many times and been on tours and everything. ... just last October we did a series of concerts in Europe, Curtis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
